purpose: Verify the expanded routing table (5 conceptual + 7 rule-level + universal checks) correctly routes intents to the right reference. Specifically tests the new rule-level files and the boundary between conceptual and rule-level references.
changes_since_iteration_1: Added universal-checks tier to SKILL.md body
Added per-reference Read this when... headers
Split vercel-rules.md into 7 new thematic rule-level references
Routing table now has 12 entries split into Conceptual and Rule-level tiers

evals.name: waterfalls-sequential-awaits
bundle-optimization-rules
evals.prompt: I have a heavy MarkdownEditor component that's only used when users click 'Edit'. Currently I import it at the top of CreatePost. Should I keep it that way or change something? What's the rule for components like this?

evals.name: server-rsc-dedup
evals.prompt: In my Next.js App Router app, both <Header /> and <Sidebar /> fetch the same user via getUser(). Two database queries per page. How do I dedupe within a single render?

evals.name: client-fetching-listeners
evals.prompt: I have ~30 components on my page each registering their own window.addEventListener('scroll', ...) for various tracking purposes. Performance is degrading. What's the pattern to fix this?

evals.name: re-render-rule-specific
evals.prompt: Code review question: should I wrap `items.length > 0` in useMemo? What about `firstName + ' ' + lastName`? My team has been wrapping everything in useMemo and I think it's wrong.

evals.name: re-renders-conceptual
evals.prompt: I wrapped my ProductList in React.memo but it still re-renders every time the parent updates. Same products array, same callbacks. What's the underlying reason — is React.memo not working?

evals.name: rendering-perf-hydration
evals.prompt: My <Timestamp date={new Date()} /> shows a hydration mismatch warning AND causes a layout flash because the timestamp briefly shows the server value then jumps to the client value. How do I fix both?

evals.name: javascript-perf-hot-loop
